commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=bdbffb65d5b0d5abe2cced31d744c8f941c1b67e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds hidden pre-validation `sessions_send` normalization for `SendMessage`, `content`, and `text`, strips formatted reasoning from alias bodies, adds focused regression tests, and adds a `CHANGELOG.md` entry.

PR surface: Source +25, Tests +114, Docs +1. Total +140 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main validates `sessions_send` against a schema that requires `message`, and the tool has no pre-validation normalizer. A model call using `SendMessage` therefore fails before `execute` can deliver the message.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers are live proof and maintainer acceptance of a cross-session hidden-alias boundary, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(agents): normalize sessions_send message aliases
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep the model-facing `sessions_send` schema canonical: `message` remains required and aliases are not advertised.
- Add hidden pre-validation normalization for model-emitted body aliases: `SendMessage`, `content`, and `text`.
- Reuse the same normalization at direct execution time so manual/test callers behave the same way.
- Strip formatted-reasoning content from alias values before forwarding inter-session messages.
- Add regression coverage for visible schema shape, hidden argument preparation, direct alias execution, and formatted-reasoning sanitization.

Closes #88146.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `sessions_send` accepts model-emitted body aliases without exposing those aliases in the model-facing schema. Calls with `SendMessage`, `content`, or `text` are normalized to canonical `message` before validation/execution, and formatted reasoning is stripped from alias content.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on PR head `a053e2c904` after `pnpm install` hydrated missing dependencies.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts && git diff --check"`.
- **Evidence after fix:** `src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts` asserts `sessions_send` still exposes only canonical `message` in schema, keeps `message` required, hides `SendMessage`/`content`/`text`, prepares hidden aliases before validation, removes alias keys from prepared arguments, and sanitizes formatted reasoning.
- **Observed result after fix:** Targeted Vitest files passed (`30` and `35` tests). Autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings and reran the same focused tests plus `git diff --check` successfully.
- **What was not tested:** Full model-driven Anthropic/Pi A2A round trip. The previous PR proof showed direct live gateway acceptance; this rewrite preserves the same direct execution behavior while additionally hiding aliases from the public schema.
- **Target test file:** `src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts`

## Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts && git diff --check"`

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** #84079/#84102 fixed model-generated body alias normalization for the channel `message` tool, but `sessions_send` still required the canonical `message` property at its own validation boundary. Anthropic/Pi-backed agents can emit the body as `SendMessage`, so those calls failed validation before `sessions_send` could execute. The fix now normalizes known aliases before validation while keeping the public tool schema canonical.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
